commit.message:
fix: use set:html for chart data injection; add Playwright E2E tests

- Replace set:text with set:html on all <script type="application/json"> elements
  set:text HTML-encodes quotes to " which browsers do NOT decode inside
  <script> raw text elements, causing JSON.parse() to fail silently for
  every chart on the site. set:html injects verbatim content instead.

- Add Playwright E2E chart regression suite (18 tests, 3 pages):
  * Verifies all 9 JSON data payloads parse as valid JSON in-browser
  * Verifies all canvas elements have non-zero bounding boxes (Chart.js rendered)
  * Regression guards for key contracts (lowercase distros, title-case osVersionDist)
  * Verifies countme explainer callout is visible

- Add vitest.config.ts to exclude tests/e2e from unit test runner
- Add just test-e2e and just test-all targets to Justfile
- Wire E2E tests into CI (npx playwright install + npm run test:e2e)
  with failure artifact upload for playwright-report/

Root cause: Astro set:text is correct for HTML elements but wrong for
<script> elements because browsers treat script content as raw text (no
entity decoding). The fix applies to all 9 chart components across 3 pages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix moveFileUp/Down: preserve vertical formatting and fix first-press no-op (#1499)

* Fix moveFileUp/Down breaking vertical formatting and requiring two presses

The root cause is that XmlDocument with PreserveWhitespace=true inserts
whitespace-only text nodes between XML elements. The original code used
node.PreviousSibling / node.NextSibling to find adjacent Compile elements,
but those return the intervening whitespace text nodes rather than the
actual <Compile> elements.

This caused two bugs:
1. The first button press made no visible change to compile order, because
   InsertBefore(node, whitespaceTextNode) placed the node adjacent to the
   whitespace—leaving the logical Compile order unchanged.
2. The second press actually moved the element, but produced 'horizontal'
   formatting (<Compile A /><Compile B /> on one line) because the
   reinserted element lacked a preceding whitespace text node.

Fix: add previousSiblingElement/nextSiblingElement helpers that skip text
nodes to find the true adjacent <Compile> elements. Then swap positions
using a clone-and-replace strategy: clone both elements, insert the clones
in swapped positions, and remove the originals. The whitespace text nodes
(indentation) are never touched, so vertical formatting is always preserved.

Fixes #1498

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* Address PR review: skip non-Compile siblings, null-guard file lookup, fix temp-file leak

- previousSiblingElement/nextSiblingElement now skip non-Compile elements
  (not just whitespace text nodes), so moveFileUp/moveFileDown won't swap
  a <Compile> node with <None> or <Content> siblings
- moveFileUp/moveFileDown guard against null itemGroup/node (missing file)
  instead of throwing NullReferenceException
- createTempFsproj/createTempFsprojCrlf use Path.GetRandomFileName() to
  avoid leaking the .tmp file created by Path.GetTempFileName()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
fix: net_r_realized includes full trade outcome + engine-specific PTP params

Two bugs in simulate_trade_outcome():

1. net_r_realized = realized_r (PTP portion only)
   Should be total_r (PTP + remaining position result)
   Effect: losses showed -0.05R (commission only) instead of -1.05R
           wins showed 0.45R (PTP) instead of actual total outcome
   Fix: replace round(realized_r,3) -> round(total_r,3) on all exit paths

2. PTP params hardcoded to engine_params['judas_swing']
   Should use signal.engine_name to get correct engine's partial_tp_rr/vol
   Effect: ict_enigma (ptp_rr=1.75) and ict_venom (ptp_rr=1.0) both used
           judas_swing defaults instead of their optimizer-tuned values
   Fix: ep = settings.engine_params.get(signal.engine_name, {})

commit.message:
fix: move E2E tests inside src/ for proper @playwright/test module resolution

Tests in repo root tests/ can't resolve @playwright/test from src/node_modules
because Node module resolution doesn't cross into sibling directories.
Moving tests to src/tests/ fixes this — tests run with 'cd src && npx playwright test'
and module resolution finds node_modules/ naturally.

Also update playwright.config.ts testDir to './tests' (src-relative).

castrojo/powerlevel: Destiny 2-themed GitHub Copilot stats site

## Architecture
- Go CLI (cmd/pl) replaces powerlevel.py — PL = 100 + sum(65 weapons)/8
- internal/data: model, loader, PL formula, stat scaling — fully unit tested
- internal/renderer: terminal ANSI output — fully unit tested
- internal/exporter: sanitized JSON for website — tested
- data/: source-of-truth JSON (powerlevel-data.json, triumphs.json, seals.json)
- src/: Astro 5 static site, GH Pages deployment

## Five Subclasses
- ⚡ Arc VELOCITY: planning speed, TDD cycles (blueprint-mode, tdd-*)
- 🔥 Solar COMMUNITY: knowledge compounding, open source presence
- 🟣 Void MASTERY: improving the toolset itself
- 🟢 Strand DISTRIBUTION: packaging, getting software to users
- 🔵 Stasis STABILITY: OCI builds, LTS, platform infrastructure

## Ten Supers (Agents)
Thundercrash · Fists of Havoc (×3) · Hammer of Sol · Burning Maul
Sentinel Shield · Ward of Dawn · Bladefury · Glacial Quake

## 65 Weapons (Skills) mapped to iconic D2 guns
Primary weapon: Trustee (workflow skill) — Deep Stone Crypt Scout Rifle

## Six Memory Stats (auto-computed from session history)
RECALL · ENDURANCE · SYNTHESIS · BREADTH · FORESIGHT · OUTPUT
Log-scaled 0-100. Soft cap ~250 sessions, Pinnacle after years.

## Season of the Powerlevel — Q1 2026

commit.message:
Melissa/fix/copy board fixes (#97)

* Update templates and add OBF patch for compliance

- Enhanced accessibility by correcting label `for` attributes in various templates (register.hbs, user/edit.hbs, support.hbs, organization/reports.hbs).
- Improved download button tooltips in download-board.hbs and components/download-board.hbs for better user guidance.
- Added a new OBF compliance patch in lib/obf_lingolinq_patch.rb to address custom attribute handling.
- Updated .env.example to include a new configuration for OBF_LINGOLINQ_PATCH.
- Introduced presigned URL functionality in Uploader for secure file downloads, ensuring compatibility with S3 bucket policies.
- Documented accessibility label violations and fixes in ACCESSIBILITY_LABEL_VIOLATIONS.md.

* Enhance logging and add health check endpoint

- Updated logging in ApplicationController to avoid logging sensitive token values, indicating only their presence or absence.
- Modified token response logging in SessionController to log only non-sensitive metadata.
- Introduced a new health check endpoint in SessionController to verify database and Redis connectivity, with appropriate error handling.

* Refine user tracking logic to include supporter registration check

- Updated the `track_new_user` and `persist_new_user` methods in ExternalTracker to ensure that only users with a supporter registration type (therapists, teachers, admins, purchasers) are tracked.
- Enhanced tests to verify that communicator accounts do not trigger user tracking, ensuring compliance with data handling policies.

* Update Google Translate TTS API endpoint to use HTTPS for improved security

* Update render configuration and add health check route

- Changed the branch in render.yaml from 'functional-baseline' to 'main' and added a health check path.
- Introduced a new health check route in routes.rb to enhance system monitoring capabilities.

commit.message:
feat: add log source ingest ralph with Seq backend (#643)

* Initial plan

* feat: add log source ingest ralph with Seq backend

Add a new log_source module (modeled after the kanban integration) that
allows a ralph to use structured log platforms like Seq as a source of
ingest. Log entries matching a configurable filter are queried and
automatically converted into wreck-it tasks for agents to triage and fix.

- LogSourceConfig added to Config with provider, api_token, api_base_url,
  filter, and max_entries fields
- LogSourceProvider trait with query_entries() method
- SeqProvider implementation using Seq REST API (/api/events)
- LogSourceClient enum dispatch wrapper
- LOG_SOURCE_LABEL_PREFIX for de-duplication of imported entries
- sync_log_source_inbound() in ralph_loop runs each iteration
- 21 new tests covering config, serde, provider factory, and Seq HTTP

commit.message:
test: add shared fixtures and improve test infrastructure (#105)

- Add project-wide tests/conftest.py with common path fixtures,
  seed reset, mocks, and skip marks (skip_if_missing_testdata,
  skip_if_no_gpu)
- Implement --use-gpu pytest option via pytest_addoption and
  use_gpu fixture so the gpu_compatible marker is functional
- Guard all CUDA calls in reset_seed behind torch.cuda.is_available()
  to prevent failures in CPU-only environments
- Add CLI-specific tests/mussel/cli/conftest.py with test_data_path_cli
  alias fixture
- Register gpu_compatible marker in pyproject.toml

Addresses review comments:
- No unused heavy imports at collection time
- --use-gpu flag properly registered via pytest_addoption
- CUDA API calls guarded for CPU-only torch installs

commit.message:
Potential fix for pacman datetime in list_installed_packages.py

parse_any_datetime_to_epoch() doesn’t currently handle pacman log timestamps like YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (no seconds / no timezone), which are shown in the examples above. This will cause those entries to be skipped (ts=None) and packages won’t appear in the output. Consider extending the parser to accept %Y-%m-%d %H:%M (and optionally %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S) without %z, treating naive times as local time (or explicitly documenting the assumption).

fix: WebAuthn registration for iOS passkeys (Face ID)

- Set ResidentKey to 'preferred' for discoverable credential support
  (required for iOS Passwords/Keychain passkey storage)
- Auto-detect request origin and add to RPOrigins dynamically so
  the origin validation passes when accessing from mobile devices
- Support comma-separated WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN for multiple origins
- Pre-read request body to prevent double-consumption issue
- Include transports and authenticatorAttachment in attestation response
- Add structured error logging for WebAuthn ceremonies
